revision.comment: Bot: created missing page [[Talk:Amatsu-Mikaboshi]] redirect to enwiki [[Talk:Amatsu-Mikaboshi]]
Copying from [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] to [[Category:qq]]{{Hidden category}}[[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Text replacement - "Automatic wikipedia redirects" to "Automatic wikipedia redirects]][[Category:pages to be overwritten]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects"
revision.text: #redirect[[:en:Talk:Amatsu-Mikaboshi]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]][[Category:pages to be overwritten]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:qq]]{{Hidden category}}[[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: remove deleted category Wikipedia overwrites
Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:天津甕星, en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text:
{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:22, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/56637213 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:22, 25 February 2026 (UTC) http://naosite.lb.nagasaki-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/10069/33156/1/kyoikuJK00_44_07.pdf に詳細があるが独自研究となるのか --[[利用者:河童工房|河童工房]]（[[利用者‐会話:河童工房|会話]]） 2015年8月26日 (水) 09:50 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1204782636 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:22, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Japan|myth=yes|shinto=yes|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Mythology|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Religion|importance=mid}} }} ==Untitled== I seriously doubt the accuracy of this article. It cites no sources and shares no similarities with its Japanese version. Accordings to that one, Mikaboshi is referenced in the nihonshoki, where he appears as a regular kami. It would be interesting to know where the "dark force" interpretation stems from. It appears to be profoundly Christian-influenced and does not at all fit a typological archetype of japanese Religion, neither in Shintoist, nor Buddhist terms. [[User:138.246.7.151|138.246.7.151]] 10:45, 18 May 2007 (UTC) Also, this line seems a little unfounded:
The character of the Dark One from the Wheel of Time series seems to bear resemblence to Mikaboshi, in that he is an impersonal personificaltion of primordial evil, as opposed to a single individual regarded as the source of all suffering.
Namely because this aspect of impersonal "evil" or chaotic force that drives instinctive evil is ''hardly'' specific to Japanese or even Asian religion or philosophy. --[[User:Col.clawhammer|Col.clawhammer]] 09:22, 10 June 2007 (UTC) : Yes. I did some inquiry (not much until now), and found out that the concept of "evil" as it originates from man is in Shintô attributed to a set of kami that was, according to the kojiki, created from the dirt of yomi when Izanagi washed himself after returning from the underworld. Mikaboshi seems to be unrelated (though it may be possible that he holds a special place in onmyôdô). I think I will try to rewrite this article after some more research. [[User:87.163.117.162|87.163.117.162]] 22:46, 11 June 2007 (UTC) :: Well, I ''think'' there is some legitimacy to the "primordial evil" aspect of Amatsu-Mikaboshi, but I'm recalling something from a class I had a long time ago, so I really can't say knowledgeably about it. But the concept of an inherently "evil" force, usually in contrast to a "good" force is a common concept... I asked my Japanese friend to read the Japanese page for clarification but she said it was pretty difficult to understand; not sure if she meant it was a difficult concept or a bad article, though. If you can find some research material, please let us know, I'm interested in following this up, too. --[[User:Col.clawhammer|Col.clawhammer]] 09:25, 14 June 2007 (UTC) From reading the Japanese Wikipedia page, the English one seems to be mostly unsourced nonsense. I would recommend erasing everything but the stub until further work can be done. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/219.75.242.169|219.75.242.169]] ([[User talk:219.75.242.169|talk]]) 04:29, 7 October 2010 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:22, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Title -- Proposed move == I'd like to propose moving this to [[Ama-tsu-Mikaboshi]]. As {{User|Mps}} notes in the summary for [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Amatsu-Mikaboshi&diff=563925617&oldid=563400087 this edit], the ''tsu'' in the name is the Old Japanese possessive particle. Articles containing the modern Japanese possessive particle ''no'' generally hyphenate before and after, so we should do likewise for ''tsu'' for consistency's sake. -- [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │ ''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 21:41, 12 July 2013 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:22, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == I'm confused == On the one hand this is one of the Earthly Kami conquered by the Heavenly Kami. Bu one name given for him seems indistinguishable from the the first Three Kami of Creation.--[[User:JaredMithrandir|JaredMithrandir]] ([[User talk:JaredMithrandir|talk]]) 08:44, 19 May 2016 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:22, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Problematic sources == Coulter ([https://books.google.com/books?id=VWxekbhM1yEC&lpg=PT96&dq=Amatsu-Mikaboshi&pg=PT96#v=onepage&q&f=false ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities''] by Charles Russell Coulter and Patricia Turner, Routledge, 4 Jul 2013, p.96) is problematic. * That source lists Ama-tsu-Mikaboshi as a "god of evil", which is not borne out by Japanese-language sources, such as the Digital Daijisen entry visible [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%B4%A5%E7%94%95%E6%98%9F-1050765 here at Kotobank]. Ama-tsu-mikaboshi is described in Daijisen as an 悪神 (''akujin'', "malevolent god"), but that is not the same as a "god of evil", which indicates a god in charge of evilness. The ''Nihon Shoki'' source text could be interpreted to mean not even "malevolent god", but rather "bad god" → "god who won't do what we (Ama-tsu-kami) say", "god who won't submit". * The very next entry in that ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities'' is "Amatsu-Otome", described as "Japanese angels". These are not "angels", and describing these as such is misleading -- they don't have wings, they aren't born from God. A better nutshell description would be "heavenly maidens". ⇒ I suspect that Coulter does not read Japanese, and that certain details have been lost or otherwise distorted in translation. I've added a link to Google Books' copy of Aston's ''Shinto, the Way of the Gods'', which doesn't look too far off the mark. I do not have access to the ''New Larousse encyclopedia of mythology''. I have slightly reworded the initial description of this kami to align better with Japanese sources. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 23:06, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :{{re| Eirikr}} how about tag all the problematic sources with better source needed tags.[[User:CycoMa|CycoMa]] ([[User talk:CycoMa|talk]]) 23:20, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :: {{re|CycoMa}} Great idea! Only, I spend most of my time over at Wiktionary, and I'm not familiar with much of the template infrastructure here. If you know what template equates to ''"better source needed"'' tags, please add that. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 23:37, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:22, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:天津甕星, en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text:
{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:22, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/56637213 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) http://naosite.lb.nagasaki-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/10069/33156/1/kyoikuJK00_44_07.pdf に詳細があるが独自研究となるのか --[[利用者:河童工房|河童工房]]（[[利用者‐会話:河童工房|会話]]） 2015年8月26日 (水) 09:50 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1204782636 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Japan|myth=yes|shinto=yes|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Mythology|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Religion|importance=mid}} }} ==Untitled== I seriously doubt the accuracy of this article. It cites no sources and shares no similarities with its Japanese version. Accordings to that one, Mikaboshi is referenced in the nihonshoki, where he appears as a regular kami. It would be interesting to know where the "dark force" interpretation stems from. It appears to be profoundly Christian-influenced and does not at all fit a typological archetype of japanese Religion, neither in Shintoist, nor Buddhist terms. [[User:138.246.7.151|138.246.7.151]] 10:45, 18 May 2007 (UTC) Also, this line seems a little unfounded:
The character of the Dark One from the Wheel of Time series seems to bear resemblence to Mikaboshi, in that he is an impersonal personificaltion of primordial evil, as opposed to a single individual regarded as the source of all suffering.
Namely because this aspect of impersonal "evil" or chaotic force that drives instinctive evil is ''hardly'' specific to Japanese or even Asian religion or philosophy. --[[User:Col.clawhammer|Col.clawhammer]] 09:22, 10 June 2007 (UTC) : Yes. I did some inquiry (not much until now), and found out that the concept of "evil" as it originates from man is in Shintô attributed to a set of kami that was, according to the kojiki, created from the dirt of yomi when Izanagi washed himself after returning from the underworld. Mikaboshi seems to be unrelated (though it may be possible that he holds a special place in onmyôdô). I think I will try to rewrite this article after some more research. [[User:87.163.117.162|87.163.117.162]] 22:46, 11 June 2007 (UTC) :: Well, I ''think'' there is some legitimacy to the "primordial evil" aspect of Amatsu-Mikaboshi, but I'm recalling something from a class I had a long time ago, so I really can't say knowledgeably about it. But the concept of an inherently "evil" force, usually in contrast to a "good" force is a common concept... I asked my Japanese friend to read the Japanese page for clarification but she said it was pretty difficult to understand; not sure if she meant it was a difficult concept or a bad article, though. If you can find some research material, please let us know, I'm interested in following this up, too. --[[User:Col.clawhammer|Col.clawhammer]] 09:25, 14 June 2007 (UTC) From reading the Japanese Wikipedia page, the English one seems to be mostly unsourced nonsense. I would recommend erasing everything but the stub until further work can be done. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/219.75.242.169|219.75.242.169]] ([[User talk:219.75.242.169|talk]]) 04:29, 7 October 2010 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Title -- Proposed move == I'd like to propose moving this to [[Ama-tsu-Mikaboshi]]. As {{User|Mps}} notes in the summary for [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Amatsu-Mikaboshi&diff=563925617&oldid=563400087 this edit], the ''tsu'' in the name is the Old Japanese possessive particle. Articles containing the modern Japanese possessive particle ''no'' generally hyphenate before and after, so we should do likewise for ''tsu'' for consistency's sake. -- [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │ ''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 21:41, 12 July 2013 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == I'm confused == On the one hand this is one of the Earthly Kami conquered by the Heavenly Kami. Bu one name given for him seems indistinguishable from the the first Three Kami of Creation.--[[User:JaredMithrandir|JaredMithrandir]] ([[User talk:JaredMithrandir|talk]]) 08:44, 19 May 2016 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Problematic sources == Coulter ([https://books.google.com/books?id=VWxekbhM1yEC&lpg=PT96&dq=Amatsu-Mikaboshi&pg=PT96#v=onepage&q&f=false ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities''] by Charles Russell Coulter and Patricia Turner, Routledge, 4 Jul 2013, p.96) is problematic. * That source lists Ama-tsu-Mikaboshi as a "god of evil", which is not borne out by Japanese-language sources, such as the Digital Daijisen entry visible [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%B4%A5%E7%94%95%E6%98%9F-1050765 here at Kotobank]. Ama-tsu-mikaboshi is described in Daijisen as an 悪神 (''akujin'', "malevolent god"), but that is not the same as a "god of evil", which indicates a god in charge of evilness. The ''Nihon Shoki'' source text could be interpreted to mean not even "malevolent god", but rather "bad god" → "god who won't do what we (Ama-tsu-kami) say", "god who won't submit". * The very next entry in that ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities'' is "Amatsu-Otome", described as "Japanese angels". These are not "angels", and describing these as such is misleading -- they don't have wings, they aren't born from God. A better nutshell description would be "heavenly maidens". ⇒ I suspect that Coulter does not read Japanese, and that certain details have been lost or otherwise distorted in translation. I've added a link to Google Books' copy of Aston's ''Shinto, the Way of the Gods'', which doesn't look too far off the mark. I do not have access to the ''New Larousse encyclopedia of mythology''. I have slightly reworded the initial description of this kami to align better with Japanese sources. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 23:06, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :{{re| Eirikr}} how about tag all the problematic sources with better source needed tags.[[User:CycoMa|CycoMa]] ([[User talk:CycoMa|talk]]) 23:20, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :: {{re|CycoMa}} Great idea! Only, I spend most of my time over at Wiktionary, and I'm not familiar with much of the template infrastructure here. If you know what template equates to ''"better source needed"'' tags, please add that. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 23:37, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:天津甕星, en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text:
{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:22, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/56637213 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) http://naosite.lb.nagasaki-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/10069/33156/1/kyoikuJK00_44_07.pdf に詳細があるが独自研究となるのか --[[利用者:河童工房|河童工房]]（[[利用者‐会話:河童工房|会話]]） 2015年8月26日 (水) 09:50 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1204782636 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Japan|myth=yes|shinto=yes|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Mythology|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Religion|importance=mid}} }} ==Untitled== I seriously doubt the accuracy of this article. It cites no sources and shares no similarities with its Japanese version. Accordings to that one, Mikaboshi is referenced in the nihonshoki, where he appears as a regular kami. It would be interesting to know where the "dark force" interpretation stems from. It appears to be profoundly Christian-influenced and does not at all fit a typological archetype of japanese Religion, neither in Shintoist, nor Buddhist terms. [[User:138.246.7.151|138.246.7.151]] 10:45, 18 May 2007 (UTC) Also, this line seems a little unfounded:
The character of the Dark One from the Wheel of Time series seems to bear resemblence to Mikaboshi, in that he is an impersonal personificaltion of primordial evil, as opposed to a single individual regarded as the source of all suffering.
Namely because this aspect of impersonal "evil" or chaotic force that drives instinctive evil is ''hardly'' specific to Japanese or even Asian religion or philosophy. --[[User:Col.clawhammer|Col.clawhammer]] 09:22, 10 June 2007 (UTC) : Yes. I did some inquiry (not much until now), and found out that the concept of "evil" as it originates from man is in Shintô attributed to a set of kami that was, according to the kojiki, created from the dirt of yomi when Izanagi washed himself after returning from the underworld. Mikaboshi seems to be unrelated (though it may be possible that he holds a special place in onmyôdô). I think I will try to rewrite this article after some more research. [[User:87.163.117.162|87.163.117.162]] 22:46, 11 June 2007 (UTC) :: Well, I ''think'' there is some legitimacy to the "primordial evil" aspect of Amatsu-Mikaboshi, but I'm recalling something from a class I had a long time ago, so I really can't say knowledgeably about it. But the concept of an inherently "evil" force, usually in contrast to a "good" force is a common concept... I asked my Japanese friend to read the Japanese page for clarification but she said it was pretty difficult to understand; not sure if she meant it was a difficult concept or a bad article, though. If you can find some research material, please let us know, I'm interested in following this up, too. --[[User:Col.clawhammer|Col.clawhammer]] 09:25, 14 June 2007 (UTC) From reading the Japanese Wikipedia page, the English one seems to be mostly unsourced nonsense. I would recommend erasing everything but the stub until further work can be done. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/219.75.242.169|219.75.242.169]] ([[User talk:219.75.242.169|talk]]) 04:29, 7 October 2010 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Title -- Proposed move == I'd like to propose moving this to [[Ama-tsu-Mikaboshi]]. As {{User|Mps}} notes in the summary for [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Amatsu-Mikaboshi&diff=563925617&oldid=563400087 this edit], the ''tsu'' in the name is the Old Japanese possessive particle. Articles containing the modern Japanese possessive particle ''no'' generally hyphenate before and after, so we should do likewise for ''tsu'' for consistency's sake. -- [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │ ''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 21:41, 12 July 2013 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == I'm confused == On the one hand this is one of the Earthly Kami conquered by the Heavenly Kami. Bu one name given for him seems indistinguishable from the the first Three Kami of Creation.--[[User:JaredMithrandir|JaredMithrandir]] ([[User talk:JaredMithrandir|talk]]) 08:44, 19 May 2016 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Problematic sources == Coulter ([https://books.google.com/books?id=VWxekbhM1yEC&lpg=PT96&dq=Amatsu-Mikaboshi&pg=PT96#v=onepage&q&f=false ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities''] by Charles Russell Coulter and Patricia Turner, Routledge, 4 Jul 2013, p.96) is problematic. * That source lists Ama-tsu-Mikaboshi as a "god of evil", which is not borne out by Japanese-language sources, such as the Digital Daijisen entry visible [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%B4%A5%E7%94%95%E6%98%9F-1050765 here at Kotobank]. Ama-tsu-mikaboshi is described in Daijisen as an 悪神 (''akujin'', "malevolent god"), but that is not the same as a "god of evil", which indicates a god in charge of evilness. The ''Nihon Shoki'' source text could be interpreted to mean not even "malevolent god", but rather "bad god" → "god who won't do what we (Ama-tsu-kami) say", "god who won't submit". * The very next entry in that ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities'' is "Amatsu-Otome", described as "Japanese angels". These are not "angels", and describing these as such is misleading -- they don't have wings, they aren't born from God. A better nutshell description would be "heavenly maidens". ⇒ I suspect that Coulter does not read Japanese, and that certain details have been lost or otherwise distorted in translation. I've added a link to Google Books' copy of Aston's ''Shinto, the Way of the Gods'', which doesn't look too far off the mark. I do not have access to the ''New Larousse encyclopedia of mythology''. I have slightly reworded the initial description of this kami to align better with Japanese sources. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 23:06, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :{{re| Eirikr}} how about tag all the problematic sources with better source needed tags.[[User:CycoMa|CycoMa]] ([[User talk:CycoMa|talk]]) 23:20, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :: {{re|CycoMa}} Great idea! Only, I spend most of my time over at Wiktionary, and I'm not familiar with much of the template infrastructure here. If you know what template equates to ''"better source needed"'' tags, please add that. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 23:37, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:天津甕星, en:Amatsu-Mikaboshi); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text:
{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:22, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/56637213 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) http://naosite.lb.nagasaki-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/10069/33156/1/kyoikuJK00_44_07.pdf に詳細があるが独自研究となるのか --[[利用者:河童工房|河童工房]]（[[利用者‐会話:河童工房|会話]]） 2015年8月26日 (水) 09:50 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1204782636 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Japan|myth=yes|shinto=yes|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Mythology|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Religion|importance=mid}} }} ==Untitled== I seriously doubt the accuracy of this article. It cites no sources and shares no similarities with its Japanese version. Accordings to that one, Mikaboshi is referenced in the nihonshoki, where he appears as a regular kami. It would be interesting to know where the "dark force" interpretation stems from. It appears to be profoundly Christian-influenced and does not at all fit a typological archetype of japanese Religion, neither in Shintoist, nor Buddhist terms. [[User:138.246.7.151|138.246.7.151]] 10:45, 18 May 2007 (UTC) Also, this line seems a little unfounded:
The character of the Dark One from the Wheel of Time series seems to bear resemblence to Mikaboshi, in that he is an impersonal personificaltion of primordial evil, as opposed to a single individual regarded as the source of all suffering.
Namely because this aspect of impersonal "evil" or chaotic force that drives instinctive evil is ''hardly'' specific to Japanese or even Asian religion or philosophy. --[[User:Col.clawhammer|Col.clawhammer]] 09:22, 10 June 2007 (UTC) : Yes. I did some inquiry (not much until now), and found out that the concept of "evil" as it originates from man is in Shintô attributed to a set of kami that was, according to the kojiki, created from the dirt of yomi when Izanagi washed himself after returning from the underworld. Mikaboshi seems to be unrelated (though it may be possible that he holds a special place in onmyôdô). I think I will try to rewrite this article after some more research. [[User:87.163.117.162|87.163.117.162]] 22:46, 11 June 2007 (UTC) :: Well, I ''think'' there is some legitimacy to the "primordial evil" aspect of Amatsu-Mikaboshi, but I'm recalling something from a class I had a long time ago, so I really can't say knowledgeably about it. But the concept of an inherently "evil" force, usually in contrast to a "good" force is a common concept... I asked my Japanese friend to read the Japanese page for clarification but she said it was pretty difficult to understand; not sure if she meant it was a difficult concept or a bad article, though. If you can find some research material, please let us know, I'm interested in following this up, too. --[[User:Col.clawhammer|Col.clawhammer]] 09:25, 14 June 2007 (UTC) From reading the Japanese Wikipedia page, the English one seems to be mostly unsourced nonsense. I would recommend erasing everything but the stub until further work can be done. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/219.75.242.169|219.75.242.169]] ([[User talk:219.75.242.169|talk]]) 04:29, 7 October 2010 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Title -- Proposed move == I'd like to propose moving this to [[Ama-tsu-Mikaboshi]]. As {{User|Mps}} notes in the summary for [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Amatsu-Mikaboshi&diff=563925617&oldid=563400087 this edit], the ''tsu'' in the name is the Old Japanese possessive particle. Articles containing the modern Japanese possessive particle ''no'' generally hyphenate before and after, so we should do likewise for ''tsu'' for consistency's sake. -- [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │ ''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 21:41, 12 July 2013 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == I'm confused == On the one hand this is one of the Earthly Kami conquered by the Heavenly Kami. Bu one name given for him seems indistinguishable from the the first Three Kami of Creation.--[[User:JaredMithrandir|JaredMithrandir]] ([[User talk:JaredMithrandir|talk]]) 08:44, 19 May 2016 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Problematic sources == Coulter ([https://books.google.com/books?id=VWxekbhM1yEC&lpg=PT96&dq=Amatsu-Mikaboshi&pg=PT96#v=onepage&q&f=false ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities''] by Charles Russell Coulter and Patricia Turner, Routledge, 4 Jul 2013, p.96) is problematic. * That source lists Ama-tsu-Mikaboshi as a "god of evil", which is not borne out by Japanese-language sources, such as the Digital Daijisen entry visible [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%B4%A5%E7%94%95%E6%98%9F-1050765 here at Kotobank]. Ama-tsu-mikaboshi is described in Daijisen as an 悪神 (''akujin'', "malevolent god"), but that is not the same as a "god of evil", which indicates a god in charge of evilness. The ''Nihon Shoki'' source text could be interpreted to mean not even "malevolent god", but rather "bad god" → "god who won't do what we (Ama-tsu-kami) say", "god who won't submit". * The very next entry in that ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities'' is "Amatsu-Otome", described as "Japanese angels". These are not "angels", and describing these as such is misleading -- they don't have wings, they aren't born from God. A better nutshell description would be "heavenly maidens". ⇒ I suspect that Coulter does not read Japanese, and that certain details have been lost or otherwise distorted in translation. I've added a link to Google Books' copy of Aston's ''Shinto, the Way of the Gods'', which doesn't look too far off the mark. I do not have access to the ''New Larousse encyclopedia of mythology''. I have slightly reworded the initial description of this kami to align better with Japanese sources. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 23:06, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :{{re| Eirikr}} how about tag all the problematic sources with better source needed tags.[[User:CycoMa|CycoMa]] ([[User talk:CycoMa|talk]]) 23:20, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :: {{re|CycoMa}} Great idea! Only, I spend most of my time over at Wiktionary, and I'm not familiar with much of the template infrastructure here. If you know what template equates to ''"better source needed"'' tags, please add that. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 23:37, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:22, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/56637213 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) http://naosite.lb.nagasaki-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/10069/33156/1/kyoikuJK00_44_07.pdf に詳細があるが独自研究となるのか --[[User:河童工房|河童工房]]（[[利用者‐会話:河童工房|会話]]） 2015年8月26日 (水) 09:50 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1204782636 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Japan|myth=yes|shinto=yes|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Mythology|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Religion|importance=mid}} }} ==Untitled== I seriously doubt the accuracy of this article. It cites no sources and shares no similarities with its Japanese version. Accordings to that one, Mikaboshi is referenced in the nihonshoki, where he appears as a regular kami. It would be interesting to know where the "dark force" interpretation stems from. It appears to be profoundly Christian-influenced and does not at all fit a typological archetype of japanese Religion, neither in Shintoist, nor Buddhist terms. [[User:138.246.7.151|138.246.7.151]] 10:45, 18 May 2007 (UTC) Also, this line seems a little unfounded:
The character of the Dark One from the Wheel of Time series seems to bear resemblence to Mikaboshi, in that he is an impersonal personificaltion of primordial evil, as opposed to a single individual regarded as the source of all suffering.
Namely because this aspect of impersonal "evil" or chaotic force that drives instinctive evil is ''hardly'' specific to Japanese or even Asian religion or philosophy. --[[User:Col.clawhammer|Col.clawhammer]] 09:22, 10 June 2007 (UTC) : Yes. I did some inquiry (not much until now), and found out that the concept of "evil" as it originates from man is in Shintô attributed to a set of kami that was, according to the kojiki, created from the dirt of yomi when Izanagi washed himself after returning from the underworld. Mikaboshi seems to be unrelated (though it may be possible that he holds a special place in onmyôdô). I think I will try to rewrite this article after some more research. [[User:87.163.117.162|87.163.117.162]] 22:46, 11 June 2007 (UTC) :: Well, I ''think'' there is some legitimacy to the "primordial evil" aspect of Amatsu-Mikaboshi, but I'm recalling something from a class I had a long time ago, so I really can't say knowledgeably about it. But the concept of an inherently "evil" force, usually in contrast to a "good" force is a common concept... I asked my Japanese friend to read the Japanese page for clarification but she said it was pretty difficult to understand; not sure if she meant it was a difficult concept or a bad article, though. If you can find some research material, please let us know, I'm interested in following this up, too. --[[User:Col.clawhammer|Col.clawhammer]] 09:25, 14 June 2007 (UTC) From reading the Japanese Wikipedia page, the English one seems to be mostly unsourced nonsense. I would recommend erasing everything but the stub until further work can be done. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/219.75.242.169|219.75.242.169]] ([[User talk:219.75.242.169|talk]]) 04:29, 7 October 2010 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Title -- Proposed move == I'd like to propose moving this to [[Ama-tsu-Mikaboshi]]. As {{User|Mps}} notes in the summary for [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Amatsu-Mikaboshi&diff=563925617&oldid=563400087 this edit], the ''tsu'' in the name is the Old Japanese possessive particle. Articles containing the modern Japanese possessive particle ''no'' generally hyphenate before and after, so we should do likewise for ''tsu'' for consistency's sake. -- [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │ ''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 21:41, 12 July 2013 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == I'm confused == On the one hand this is one of the Earthly Kami conquered by the Heavenly Kami. Bu one name given for him seems indistinguishable from the the first Three Kami of Creation.--[[User:JaredMithrandir|JaredMithrandir]] ([[User talk:JaredMithrandir|talk]]) 08:44, 19 May 2016 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Problematic sources == Coulter ([https://books.google.com/books?id=VWxekbhM1yEC&lpg=PT96&dq=Amatsu-Mikaboshi&pg=PT96#v=onepage&q&f=false ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities''] by Charles Russell Coulter and Patricia Turner, Routledge, 4 Jul 2013, p.96) is problematic. * That source lists Ama-tsu-Mikaboshi as a "god of evil", which is not borne out by Japanese-language sources, such as the Digital Daijisen entry visible [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%B4%A5%E7%94%95%E6%98%9F-1050765 here at Kotobank]. Ama-tsu-mikaboshi is described in Daijisen as an 悪神 (''akujin'', "malevolent god"), but that is not the same as a "god of evil", which indicates a god in charge of evilness. The ''Nihon Shoki'' source text could be interpreted to mean not even "malevolent god", but rather "bad god" → "god who won't do what we (Ama-tsu-kami) say", "god who won't submit". * The very next entry in that ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities'' is "Amatsu-Otome", described as "Japanese angels". These are not "angels", and describing these as such is misleading -- they don't have wings, they aren't born from God. A better nutshell description would be "heavenly maidens". ⇒ I suspect that Coulter does not read Japanese, and that certain details have been lost or otherwise distorted in translation. I've added a link to Google Books' copy of Aston's ''Shinto, the Way of the Gods'', which doesn't look too far off the mark. I do not have access to the ''New Larousse encyclopedia of mythology''. I have slightly reworded the initial description of this kami to align better with Japanese sources. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 23:06, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :{{re| Eirikr}} how about tag all the problematic sources with better source needed tags.[[User:CycoMa|CycoMa]] ([[User talk:CycoMa|talk]]) 23:20, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :: {{re|CycoMa}} Great idea! Only, I spend most of my time over at Wiktionary, and I'm not familiar with much of the template infrastructure here. If you know what template equates to ''"better source needed"'' tags, please add that. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 23:37, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Amatsu-Mikaboshi here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:38, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:22, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/56637213 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) http://naosite.lb.nagasaki-u.ac.jp/dspace/bitstream/10069/33156/1/kyoikuJK00_44_07.pdf に詳細があるが独自研究となるのか --[[User:河童工房|河童工房]]（[[利用者‐会話:河童工房|会話]]） 2015年8月26日 (水) 09:50 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1204782636 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Japan|myth=yes|shinto=yes|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Mythology|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Religion|importance=mid}} }} ==Untitled== I seriously doubt the accuracy of this article. It cites no sources and shares no similarities with its Japanese version. Accordings to that one, Mikaboshi is referenced in the nihonshoki, where he appears as a regular kami. It would be interesting to know where the "dark force" interpretation stems from. It appears to be profoundly Christian-influenced and does not at all fit a typological archetype of japanese Religion, neither in Shintoist, nor Buddhist terms. [[User:138.246.7.151|138.246.7.151]] 10:45, 18 May 2007 (UTC) Also, this line seems a little unfounded:
The character of the Dark One from the Wheel of Time series seems to bear resemblence to Mikaboshi, in that he is an impersonal personificaltion of primordial evil, as opposed to a single individual regarded as the source of all suffering.
Namely because this aspect of impersonal "evil" or chaotic force that drives instinctive evil is ''hardly'' specific to Japanese or even Asian religion or philosophy. --[[User:Col.clawhammer|Col.clawhammer]] 09:22, 10 June 2007 (UTC) : Yes. I did some inquiry (not much until now), and found out that the concept of "evil" as it originates from man is in Shintô attributed to a set of kami that was, according to the kojiki, created from the dirt of yomi when Izanagi washed himself after returning from the underworld. Mikaboshi seems to be unrelated (though it may be possible that he holds a special place in onmyôdô). I think I will try to rewrite this article after some more research. [[User:87.163.117.162|87.163.117.162]] 22:46, 11 June 2007 (UTC) :: Well, I ''think'' there is some legitimacy to the "primordial evil" aspect of Amatsu-Mikaboshi, but I'm recalling something from a class I had a long time ago, so I really can't say knowledgeably about it. But the concept of an inherently "evil" force, usually in contrast to a "good" force is a common concept... I asked my Japanese friend to read the Japanese page for clarification but she said it was pretty difficult to understand; not sure if she meant it was a difficult concept or a bad article, though. If you can find some research material, please let us know, I'm interested in following this up, too. --[[User:Col.clawhammer|Col.clawhammer]] 09:25, 14 June 2007 (UTC) From reading the Japanese Wikipedia page, the English one seems to be mostly unsourced nonsense. I would recommend erasing everything but the stub until further work can be done. —Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|unsigned]] comment added by [[Special:Contributions/219.75.242.169|219.75.242.169]] ([[User talk:219.75.242.169|talk]]) 04:29, 7 October 2010 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Title -- Proposed move == I'd like to propose moving this to [[Ama-tsu-Mikaboshi]]. As {{User|Mps}} notes in the summary for [http://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Amatsu-Mikaboshi&diff=563925617&oldid=563400087 this edit], the ''tsu'' in the name is the Old Japanese possessive particle. Articles containing the modern Japanese possessive particle ''no'' generally hyphenate before and after, so we should do likewise for ''tsu'' for consistency's sake. -- [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │ ''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 21:41, 12 July 2013 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == I'm confused == On the one hand this is one of the Earthly Kami conquered by the Heavenly Kami. Bu one name given for him seems indistinguishable from the the first Three Kami of Creation.--[[User:JaredMithrandir|JaredMithrandir]] ([[User talk:JaredMithrandir|talk]]) 08:44, 19 May 2016 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Problematic sources == Coulter ([https://books.google.com/books?id=VWxekbhM1yEC&lpg=PT96&dq=Amatsu-Mikaboshi&pg=PT96#v=onepage&q&f=false ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities''] by Charles Russell Coulter and Patricia Turner, Routledge, 4 Jul 2013, p.96) is problematic. * That source lists Ama-tsu-Mikaboshi as a "god of evil", which is not borne out by Japanese-language sources, such as the Digital Daijisen entry visible [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%A4%A9%E6%B4%A5%E7%94%95%E6%98%9F-1050765 here at Kotobank]. Ama-tsu-mikaboshi is described in Daijisen as an 悪神 (''akujin'', "malevolent god"), but that is not the same as a "god of evil", which indicates a god in charge of evilness. The ''Nihon Shoki'' source text could be interpreted to mean not even "malevolent god", but rather "bad god" → "god who won't do what we (Ama-tsu-kami) say", "god who won't submit". * The very next entry in that ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Deities'' is "Amatsu-Otome", described as "Japanese angels". These are not "angels", and describing these as such is misleading -- they don't have wings, they aren't born from God. A better nutshell description would be "heavenly maidens". ⇒ I suspect that Coulter does not read Japanese, and that certain details have been lost or otherwise distorted in translation. I've added a link to Google Books' copy of Aston's ''Shinto, the Way of the Gods'', which doesn't look too far off the mark. I do not have access to the ''New Larousse encyclopedia of mythology''. I have slightly reworded the initial description of this kami to align better with Japanese sources. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 23:06, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :{{re| Eirikr}} how about tag all the problematic sources with better source needed tags.[[User:CycoMa|CycoMa]] ([[User talk:CycoMa|talk]]) 23:20, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :: {{re|CycoMa}} Great idea! Only, I spend most of my time over at Wiktionary, and I'm not familiar with much of the template infrastructure here. If you know what template equates to ''"better source needed"'' tags, please add that. ‑‑ [[User:Eirikr|Eiríkr Útlendi]] │''[[User talk:Eirikr|Tala við mig]]'' 23:37, 12 October 2021 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:41, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
